prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_itinerary_stop day_slot_primary_1)
(assign_transport_to_stop day_slot_primary_1 transport_option_1)
(attach_activity_to_stop day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_activity_assignment day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_ticket_to_primary_day_slot day_slot_primary_1 morning_time_window general_admission_ticket)
(finalize_primary_day_slot_ticket_assignment day_slot_primary_1 morning_time_window sightseeing_tour_activity general_admission_ticket)
(retract_activity_from_stop day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(create_itinerary_stop day_slot_secondary_1)
(assign_transport_to_stop day_slot_secondary_1 transport_option_2)
(attach_activity_to_stop day_slot_secondary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_activity_assignment day_slot_secondary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_ticket_to_secondary_day_slot day_slot_secondary_1 afternoon_time_window general_admission_ticket)
(finalize_secondary_day_slot_ticket_assignment day_slot_secondary_1 afternoon_time_window sightseeing_tour_activity general_admission_ticket)
(assemble_booking_candidate_basic day_slot_primary_1 day_slot_secondary_1 morning_time_window afternoon_time_window daily_booking_candidate_1)
(retract_activity_from_stop day_slot_secondary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(attach_activity_to_stop day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_booking_candidate_time_assignment daily_booking_candidate_1 day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_primary_day_slot_assignment day_slot_primary_1 daily_booking_candidate_1)
(confirm_secondary_day_slot_assignment day_slot_secondary_1 daily_booking_candidate_1)
(authorize_budget_for_stop day_slot_primary_1 budget_token_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_budget_to_primary_day_slot day_slot_primary_1 transport_option_1 budget_token_1)
(retract_activity_from_stop day_slot_primary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(attach_activity_to_stop day_slot_secondary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(authorize_budget_for_stop day_slot_secondary_1 budget_token_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_budget_to_secondary_day_slot day_slot_secondary_1 transport_option_2 budget_token_1)
(create_itinerary_stop itinerary_instance_a)
(assign_transport_to_stop itinerary_instance_a transport_option_1)
(retract_activity_from_stop day_slot_secondary_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(attach_activity_to_stop itinerary_instance_a sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_activity_assignment itinerary_instance_a sightseeing_tour_activity)
(reserve_venue_for_stop itinerary_instance_a venue_museum)
(attach_supplier_service_variant itinerary_instance_a supplier_service_variant_standard_room)
(reserve_accommodation_for_booking_candidate itinerary_instance_a hotel_option_a daily_booking_candidate_1)
(validate_accommodation_supplier_for_itinerary itinerary_instance_a hotel_option_a daily_booking_candidate_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(request_supplier_confirmation_stage2 itinerary_instance_a supplier_service_variant_standard_room venue_museum hotel_option_a daily_booking_candidate_1)
(attach_special_requirement_token itinerary_instance_a accessibility_requirement)
(request_supplier_confirmation_stage3 itinerary_instance_a accessibility_requirement hotel_option_a daily_booking_candidate_1)
(finalize_itinerary_element itinerary_instance_a)
(authorize_budget_for_stop itinerary_instance_a budget_token_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_budget_to_itinerary itinerary_instance_a transport_option_1 budget_token_1)
(release_venue_from_stop itinerary_instance_a venue_museum)
(release_supplier_service_variant itinerary_instance_a supplier_service_variant_standard_room)
(release_special_requirement_token itinerary_instance_a accessibility_requirement)
(retract_activity_from_stop itinerary_instance_a sightseeing_tour_activity)
(create_itinerary_stop itinerary_instance_b)
(assign_transport_to_stop itinerary_instance_b transport_option_2)
(attach_activity_to_stop itinerary_instance_b sightseeing_tour_activity)
(confirm_activity_assignment itinerary_instance_b sightseeing_tour_activity)
(reserve_venue_for_stop itinerary_instance_b venue_museum)
(attach_supplier_service_variant itinerary_instance_b supplier_service_variant_standard_room)
(reserve_accommodation_for_booking_candidate itinerary_instance_b hotel_option_b daily_booking_candidate_1)
(validate_accommodation_supplier_for_itinerary itinerary_instance_b hotel_option_b daily_booking_candidate_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(request_supplier_confirmation_stage2 itinerary_instance_b supplier_service_variant_standard_room venue_museum hotel_option_b daily_booking_candidate_1)
(attach_special_requirement_token itinerary_instance_b accessibility_requirement)
(request_supplier_confirmation_stage3 itinerary_instance_b accessibility_requirement hotel_option_b daily_booking_candidate_1)
(finalize_itinerary_element itinerary_instance_b)
(authorize_budget_for_stop itinerary_instance_b budget_token_1 sightseeing_tour_activity)
(allocate_budget_to_itinerary itinerary_instance_b transport_option_2 budget_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many actions and predicates. The goal is to have budget allocated to day_slot_primary_1, day_slot_secondary_1, itinerary_instance_a, and itinerary_instance_b.


## Examining Budget Allocation  
- The problem involves allocating a budget across multiple components: day_slot_primary_1, day_slot_secondary_1, itinerary_instance_a, and itinerary_instance_b.  
- The complexity arises from managing various actions and predicates to ensure proper distribution.

## Examining Budget Allocation  
- Specific actions for budget allocation include allocate_budget_to_primary_day_slot, allocate_budget_to_secondary_day_slot, and allocate_budget_to_itinerary.  
- Each action requires conditions like budget authorization, transport assignment, and available budget tokens, with effects such as marking budget as allocated.  
- Budget authorization for stops involves finalizing itinerary elements, assigning activities, and consuming budget tokens.  
- Day slots and itinerary instances must be finalized and have activities and transports assigned before budget allocation.  
- Initial state lacks initialized itinerary elements, but includes available transports, activities, venues, and tickets.  

## Identifying Initial Conditions  
- Objects include day_slot_primary_1, day_slot_secondary_1, itinerary_instances a and b, and various options like transport, activities, and accommodations.  
- Initial facts show day_slot_primary_1 assigned to morning_time_window and day_slot_secondary_1 to afternoon_time_window.  
- Both itinerary_instances a and b are associated with day_slot_primary_1 and day_slot_secondary_1, sharing daily_booking_candidate_1.  
- Additional initial conditions include available promo offers, ancillary services, and budget_token_1, with preferences assigned to each itinerary instance.

## Building Allocation Workflow  
- The process starts with finalizing, then authorizing budget, assigning transport, and finally allocating the budget.  
- For allocate_budget_to_primary_day_slot, conditions include budget authorization, transport assignment, and an available budget token, with effects releasing transport and budget token for reuse.  

## Sequencing Resource Assignments  
- With only one sightseeing_tour_activity, venues, and tickets available, they must be shared or sequenced across stops, consuming and potentially retracting availability.  
- Actions like attach_activity_to_stop and allocate_ticket_to_primary_day_slot consume resources, but retract actions allow reuse if needed.  

## Outlining Stop Initialization  
- Creating a stop initializes it, followed by assigning transport and attaching activities, leading to readiness for venue assignment.  
- Locking and readying a stop involves confirming activity, venue, and time window assignments, with multiple paths like confirm_primary_day_slot_with_venue or finalize_primary_day_slot_ticket_assignment.  

## Planning Booking Candidate Readiness  
- To finalize day slots, booking candidates must be ready and time-confirmed, achieved through actions like assemble_booking.  
- Confirming primary or secondary day slot assignments requires locked and ready slots, ensuring all prerequisites are met.

## Examining Booking Candidate Assembly  
- Assembling a booking candidate requires locked and ready primary and secondary day slots, with selected time windows, and an available booking candidate.  
- The basic assemble_booking_candidate action doesn't require time windows to have tickets, focusing instead on readiness and assignments.  

## Analyzing Ticket and Activity States  
- Allocating a ticket sets has_ticket for a time window, and finalizing doesn't remove it, leaving has_ticket true post-finalization.  
- Attaching an activity to a stop keeps it assigned unless explicitly retracted, impacting actions like confirm_booking_candidate_time_assignment.  

## Investigating Booking Confirmation Challenges  
- Confirming booking candidate time requires the primary day slot to have an activity, which may conflict with retracting it for secondary slot use.  
- Retracting an activity from the primary slot before secondary assignment could break confirmation, necessitating careful sequencing to maintain has_activity.

## Exploring Itinerary Finalization Paths  
- Finalizing an itinerary instance can follow multiple paths, such as requiring supplier_confirmation_ready or using preference validation.  
- One path involves attaching preference tokens, validating preferences and special requirements, leading to finalized status after stage 2.  

## Checking Itinerary Prerequisites  
- Ready_for_venue on itinerary instances requires initialization, transport assignment, activity attachment, and activity confirmation.  
- Itinerary instances need itinerary stops created and transport assigned, like transport_3, to proceed with venue readiness.  

## Assessing Preference Path Feasibility  
- For itinerary instance a, preference token 1 is available and already assigned, making the preference path viable.  
- For instance b, preference token 2 is unavailable, suggesting a different path like accommodation might be needed.  

## Evaluating Accommodation Path  
- Reserve accommodation for booking candidates requires ready_for_venue, available accommodation, and time-confirmed booking candidates.  
- After reserving, validating accommodation involves ensuring activity and reservation status, potentially leading to further supplier validation.

## Continuing Accommodation Validation  
- Reserving hotel_option_a for itinerary_instance_a sets it as reserved, allocated to daily_booking_candidate_1, and unavailable.  
- Validating accommodation for instance_a requires ready_venue, reserved status, and activity, leading to accom_val_passed status.  

## Advancing Supplier Confirmation  
- Requesting supplier confirmation stage 2 for instance_a requires accom_val_passed and venue availability, resulting in supplier_avail_conf.  
- Attaching accessibility_requirement as a special need and confirming stage 3 sets conf_ready without ancillary_allowed, enabling finalization.  

## Evaluating Resource Release Needs  
- For itinerary_instance_b, releasing resources like venue_museum and supplier_service_variant from instance_a is needed to make them available for b.  
- Accommodation reservations lack a direct release action, posing challenges for reusing hotel resources after finalization.

## Examining Finalization Challenges  
- Finalizing itinerary_instance_a requires releasing venue_museum, supplier_service_variant, and special tokens to make them available for b.  
- Accommodation for a remains reserved, with no direct release action, limiting resource reuse.  

## Investigating Transport Allocation  
- Assigning transport, like transport3, to itinerary_instance_a consumes availability, requiring release via allocate_budget after finalization.  
- For itinerary_instance_b, all transports (primary1, sec2, a3) are assigned, creating a bottleneck for b’s transport needs.  

## Analyzing Transport Release Logic  
- Transports are released only after allocate_budget, which requires authorizing budget and finalization, creating a circular dependency.  
- To finalize b, a transport must be assigned, but no transports are available without prior release, posing a challenge.  

## Exploring Alternative Finalization Paths  
- All finalization actions (e.g., finalize_itinerary_element, confirm_slot_assignments) require ready_venue, which needs transport assignment.  
- No clear path exists to finalize itineraries without transport, as preference and validation paths also depend on ready_venue.

## Processing Resource Release  
- Releasing venue_museum, supplier_service_variant_standard_room, and accessibility_requirement from itinerary_instance_a makes them available for b.  
- Accommodation for a remains reserved, with no release needed, streamlining resource reuse for b.  

## Executing Itinerary Setup for B  
- Creating itinerary_stop for itinerary_instance_b and assigning transport_option_2 (t2) enables further steps, confirming t2’s availability.  
- Attaching sightseeing_tour_activity to b and reserving venue_museum sets up ready_venue status, essential for validation.  

## Confirming Supplier and Finalization for B  
- Attaching accessibility_requirement and requesting supplier_confirmation_stage3 for b results in conf_ready status, mirroring a’s path.  
- Finalizing itinerary_element for b, followed by authorize_budget_for_stop and allocate_budget_to_itinerary, achieves budget_allocated status for b.  

## Reviewing Resource Utilization  
- Transport_option_3 remains unused, but all entities (a, b, p, s) achieve budget_allocated, meeting requirements.  
- Itinerary_preference_assignment was set initially but not used, indicating flexibility in the process without impacting outcomes.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
